title: $1,000 in Amazon at IPO (1997)
description: What $1,000 invested in Amazon on its IPO date would be worth today. Amazon went public at $18/share ($1.96 split-adjusted). The company survived the dot-com crash, losing ~95% of its value between 2000-2001, before becoming one of the most valuable companies in history.
dividend_note: Amazon has never paid a dividend.
max_drawdown_note: Amazon fell ~95% during the dot-com crash (1999-2001). Investors had to hold through this drawdown to realize the long-term return.
educational_note: Amazon's four splits turned 510 initial shares into 122,448 split-adjusted shares. The critical insight: investors had to survive a 95% drawdown to realize this return.
